title: ficus tinctoria subsp. swinhoei
intro: Ficus tinctoria subsp. swinhoei, also known as the Chinese mulberry or Chinese dye plant, is a deciduous plant species within the fig family (Moraceae) native to Southeast Asia and China. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ficus tinctoria subsp. swinhoei include its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves, bark, and roots of the plant are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
